'''Anthony Puletua''' (born 25 June 1979), also known by the nickname of '''"TP"''', is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] international, Puletua previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League]] competition, primarily as {{rlp|sr}} forward.

Puletua joined Hull Kingston Rovers, on loan in 2015 from the Salford Red Devils. He made 13 appearances, scoring one try for Hull Kingston Rovers, and retired on 18 September 2015.

==Background==
Puletua was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent.

==Penrith Panthers==
Puletua played his junior rugby league with the [[St Mary's Saints]] before being signed by the [[Penrith Panthers]]. Puletua made his debut for the Penrith Panthers in 1997 and played at second row in the Panthers team which defeated the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2003 NRL Grand final]]. As 2003 NRL premiers, the Panthers travelled to England to face [[Super League VIII]] champions, the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]].  Puletua played at second-row forward in the Panthers' 22–4 loss. He was captain of the team from January 2006. Puletua's brother, [[Frank Puletua|Frank]], also played for the Penrith Panthers, retiring from professional rugby league in April 2011. On 4 October 2006, TP was selected to be a part of the Perinth Panthers' Team Legends. Used to celebrate the 40th anniversary of the Panthers, Puletua was alongside Penrith legends Mark Geyer, Brad Fitler and Rhys Wesser.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2006-10-05/wesser-named-penriths-best/1278720 |title=Wesser named Penrith's best |publisher=ABC News |date=5 October 2006 |access-date=9 September 2023}}</ref>

==St Helens==
Puletua joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at the end of the 2008 season on a three-year deal.<ref name="St Helens bring in Kiwi Puletua">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7362436.stm|title=St Helens bring in Kiwi Puletua|publisher=BBC|date=23 April 2008|access-date=23 April 2008}}</ref><ref name="Dragons chasing Puletua">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23409879-23214,00.html|title=Dragons chasing Puletua|work=Fox Sports|date=21 March 2008|access-date=20 March 2008}}</ref><ref name="Penrith players in coaching meltdown">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23448847-23214,00.html|title=Penrith players in coaching meltdown|work=Fox Sports|date=29 March 2008|access-date=29 March 2008}}</ref><ref name="Lewis set to become bunny">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23472478-23214,00.html|title=Lewis set to become bunny|work=Fox Sports|date=2 April 2008|access-date=2 April 2008}}</ref>

He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Linfoot|first=Ben|title=Grand Final: Leeds Rhinos 18 St Helens 10|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_commentary/0%2C19936%2C11070_3204177%2C00.html|access-date=22 November 2019|work=Sky Sports|date=10 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091206112549/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_commentary/0,19936,11070_3204177,00.html|archive-date=6 December 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|work=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref>

He played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref>

==Salford Red Devils==
Puletua joined the [[Salford Red Devils]] for the 2014 season. He made 26 appearances for the club in 2014, but the club was later fined £5,000 for breaching the salary cap.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-red-devils-docked-points-11241493 |title=Salford Red Devils docked points for salary cap breach |publisher=Manchester Evening News |date= 25 April 2016|access-date=9 September 2023}}</ref>

==Hull KR==
[[File:Tony Puletua 2008.jpg|thumb|left|Puletua playing for Samoa]]
Puletua joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] mid-season in 2015 on a season-long loan from Salford, and announced that he would retire from the game following his spell with Hull KR.<ref>[http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/puletua-joins-robins-on-loan Puletua joins Robins on loan]. Hullkr.co.uk (26 May 2015). Retrieved on 2016-09-22.</ref>

==Representative football==
Puletua represented the New Zealand national team on 22 occasions following his début in 1998, including the 2000 World Cup. His last selection was for New Zealand against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[ANZAC Test#Results|ANZAC Test]] on 20 April 2007 as a replacement for the injured [[David Kidwell]]. After that, he switched his allegiance to [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].

Puletua was named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team#2008 World Cup squad|Samoa]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]], and played in three matches.<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=9 October 2008}}</ref> In 2009 he was named as part of the Samoan side for the [[2009 Pacific Cup|Pacific Cup]].<ref>[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/7999.php Poching names strong Samoan squad] ''therhinos.co.uk'', 6 October 2009</ref>

Puletua was called up to the Samoan squad as an injury replacement during the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]].<ref>[http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/19512/9009531/rugby-league-world-cup-samoa-call-up-new-salford-star-tony-puletua Samoa call up new Salford star Tony Puletua] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131109055913/http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/19512/9009531/rugby-league-world-cup-samoa-call-up-new-salford-star-tony-puletua |date=9 November 2013}} ''skysports.com'', 5 November 2013</ref>

==Career highlights==
* ''First Grade Debut:'' 1997 – Round 10, Penrith v [[Perth Reds]] at the [[WACA Ground|WACA]], 4 May
* ''Representative Debut:'' 1998 – Australia vs New Zealand, [[Suncorp Stadium]], 9 October
* ''Premierships:'' 2003 – member of the [[National Rugby League season 2003#Grand final|Grand final]] winning Panthers team that defeated the [[Sydney Roosters]], 18–6
